analysis: Hotspot is blocking ping-pong communication (MPI_Send/MPI_Recv) with significant wait time. Apply selected Non-Blocking I/O candidate by replacing each blocking send/recv pair with MPI_Isend/MPI_Irecv and MPI_Waitall, preserving per-rank ordering (rank0 posts send then recv; rank1 posts recv then send) and ensuring buffer reuse only after completion. Changes are localized to the exchange loop; semantics, tags, ranks, and communicator remain unchanged.
selected_candidate_target: MPI ping-pong exchange loop dominated by blocking MPI_Send/MPI_Recv waits
applied_changes_summary: Rewrote the per-iteration ping-pong to use MPI_Isend/MPI_Irecv with MPI_Waitall(2) while preserving original per-rank post order and ensuring completion before buffer reuse and checksum.
correctness_risks: Using the same buffer for simultaneous Isend and Irecv relies on MPI allowing overlapping send/recv with the same buffer only when the program does not access/modify it until completion; this code waits before reuse, matching the precondition.
If an MPI implementation has poor progress for non-blocking operations, performance may not improve; correctness should still hold.
expected_metrics: Reduced time spent blocked in communication (lower effective mpi_wait_pct in profiles)
Improved overall runtime (TIME_SEC) for latency-dominated ping-pong
